mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously when FEDFUNDS is in the accommodation zone.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices reprice downward risk.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand. When both series decline month-over-month simultaneously, equity prices fall over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward. The mechanism requires both demand components to weaken together.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline MoM in same month
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of co-decline instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above the 102.5 index level signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as profit margins expand and earnings growth accelerates.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when INDPRO > 102.5 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and a decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals strong labor market momentum. When both occur simultaneously, equity prices rise over the next 20 trading days as consumer income growth and spending expectations improve. The mechanism requires both labor indicators to move favorably together.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100k MoM AND UNRATE falls > 0.1pp
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of co-favorable instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 240 index points while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, real rates turn negative. This regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity risk premiums expand due to inflation uncertainty and policy tightening expectations.
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation surprise
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances meeting the trigger condition, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1000 thousand units signals a contraction in forward-looking construction demand and capital investment. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices reprice downward growth expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where HOUST < 1000, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above the 15900 billion chained 2017 dollars level signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as profit expectations rise and equity valuations expand.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns when GDPC1 > 15900 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
